hypothesis: Domain and experiment distribution reflects swarm's stated goal of external benefit (PHIL-4, PHIL-16b)
method: Cross-check all 49 domains against experiment counts, frontier status, and human-benefit alignment

findings: Experiment concentration mirrors self-referentiality: meta alone has 235/1196 (19.6%)
12 orphan experiment directories (belief-variants, biology, colonies, etc.) indicate naming drift between domain and experiment namespaces
Council inactive for 141 sessions — scope was too narrow (genesis-only), expanded to belief+domain+architecture governance
8 frontier-depleted domains = structural attention death; 3 (empathy, fluid-dynamics, plant-biology) left hibernating as low-value for current goals
Top-3 domains (meta, expert-swarm, nk-complexity) all self-referential; only 2 of top-10 have external grounding potential (finance, economy)
actions_taken: Created 3 new domains: epistemology (knowledge formalization), thermodynamics (entropy/dissipation), forecasting (external grounding)
Revived 5 depleted domains with new frontiers: filtering, health, quality, mathematics, distributed-systems
Expanded council from genesis-only to belief+domain+architecture governance with 20-session cadence
Filed 3 dogma challenges: PHIL-5 (unfalsifiable hurt), PHIL-11 (no human error detection), PHIL-17 (vacuous mutual swarming)

verdict: PARTIALLY SUPPORTED — domain structure exists but experiment distribution is deeply self-referential (Gini 0.626). External-facing domains (forecasting, health, finance) are underweighted relative to stated goals.
